top_models.title: Universal Sampling Denoising (USD) for noise mapping and noise removal of non-Cartesian MRI
top_models.summary: The paper proposes a universal denoising pipeline based on random-matrix-theory for non-Cartesian MRI data, demonstrating its effectiveness in both numerical phantoms and ex vivo mouse brain data.

top_models.title: Innovative MRI Denoising Using Federated and Transfer Learning
top_models.summary: This study presents a hybrid model combining transfer learning and federated learning for MRI denoising, enhancing image quality while ensuring patient data privacy.

top_models.title: Patch2Self denoising reveals a new theoretical understanding of Diffusion MRI
top_models.summary: Patch2Self, a self-supervised learning-based denoiser, improves the repeatability and conspicuity of pathology in spinal cord dMRI scans by removing assumptions on signal structure.

top_models.title: Deep Learning Based Adaptive Noise Reduction in Multi-Contrast MR Images
top_models.summary: The paper proposes a deep learning-based approach for MRI image denoising that adapts to the input noise power, outperforming state-of-the-art methods like BM3D.

top_models.title: Low field multi-contrast MRI denoising using robust PCA
top_models.summary: The paper proposes a non-local robust PCA-based method to jointly denoise multi-contrast images acquired on a low-field MRI system, demonstrating improved image quality compared to BM3D and BM4D methods.

top_models.title: Noise2Average: deep learning based denoising without high-SNR training data using iterative residual learning
top_models.summary: A novel approach called Noise2Average improves upon the Noise2Noise method for MRI denoising by employing supervised residual learning to preserve image sharpness and transfer learning for subject-specific training.

top_models.title: Unsupervised Zero-Shot MR Image Denoising via Diffusion Null-space Model
top_models.key_method: Noise transformation network and null-space diffusion sampling
top_models.summary: The paper presents an unsupervised zero-shot MRI denoising framework that combines a noise transformation network with a pretrained diffusion model to effectively remove complex real-world noise patterns while preserving diagnostic details.

top_models.title: Self-Prior Guided Mamba-UNet Networks for Medical Image Super-Resolution
top_models.summary: The paper introduces a Mamba-based UNet architecture for medical image super-resolution, leveraging a self-prior mechanism and an improved 2D-selective-scan module. This approach efficiently models long-range dependencies with linear computational complexity, outperforming existing CNN and Transformer-based methods.

top_models.title: MRI Denoising Using Native Noise
top_models.summary: This work presents a method for MRI denoising using 'native noise denoising network' (NNDnet), leveraging the inherent noise in the data rather than simulations. It achieves good performance on T1-weighted, T2-weighted images from TMRF and low-field brain imaging.

top_models.title: A Dual-Stage Denoising Method Based on Zero-Shot Learning for Low-Field MRI
top_models.summary: This paper proposes a dual-stage denoising method for low-field MRI that combines supervised and zero-shot learning to improve image quality. It demonstrates consistent performance improvements over traditional supervised methods.

top_models.title: Dual stage MRI image restoration based on blind spot denoising and hybrid attention
top_models.key_method: Global mask mapper, perceptual loss, adaptive hybrid attention, GANs
top_models.summary: This paper proposes a dual-stage model (NRAE) for MRI image restoration, focusing on blind spot denoising and hybrid attention mechanisms to improve image quality while preserving important anatomical details.

top_models.title: Coil to Coil: Self-supervised denoising using phased-array coil images
top_models.key_method: Phased-array coil data processing with N2N algorithm
top_models.summary: The Coil to Coil (C2C) method generates two noise-corrupted images from single phased-array coil data for self-supervised denoising, requiring no clean images or paired noisy images. It outperforms existing methods in both real and synthetic noised images.

top_models.title: Denoising diffusion MRI with an improved non-local principal component analysis approach
top_models.summary: The authors propose an improved 2-step non-local PCA method for denoising diffusion MRI, showing substantial improvement in image quality and DTI metric estimation compared to existing local-PCA-based methods.

top_models.title: Hybrid PCA Denoising - Improving PCA Denoising in the Presence of Spatial Correlations
top_models.key_method: Hybrid-PCA, Marchenko-Pastur distribution, random matrix theory
top_models.summary: This paper introduces Hybrid-PCA denoising, a method that combines a-priori noise variance estimation with random matrix theory to improve PCA denoising in MRI data corrupted by spatially correlated noise.

top_models.title: Noise2Noise MRI for High-resolution Diffusion-weighted Imaging of the Brain: Deep Learning-based Denoising Without Need for Highly Averaged Ground-Truth Images
top_models.summary: A deep learning denoising method called N2N-MRI is proposed for high-resolution diffusion-weighted imaging of the brain. Unlike conventional methods that require highly averaged ground-truth images, N2N-MRI uses noise2noise theory to achieve comparable performance without clean targets. The method outperformed traditional approaches in terms of maximum peak SNRs during training.

top_models.title: Non-local low rank denoising method for complex-valued DWI
top_models.summary: A two-step non-local low-rank denoising method is proposed to improve the quality of complex-valued diffusion-weighted MRI images. The method outperforms existing approaches in simulation and in vivo data, demonstrating improvements in PSNR and SSIM.

top_models.title: Self-supervised learning for denoising of multidimensional MRI data
top_models.key_method: Self-supervised model trained on noisy images
top_models.summary: The paper presents a self-supervised deep-learning framework for fast denoising of multidimensional MRI data, which does not require ground truth clean images. The method exploits redundancy in multidimensional MRI data and achieves significant improvement over previous methods in noise reduction and quantification accuracy.

top_models.title: Self-Supervised Noise Adaptive MRI Denoising via Repetition to Repetition (Rep2Rep) Learning
top_models.summary: This paper introduces Repetition to Repetition (Rep2Rep) learning, a novel self-supervised framework for MRI denoising at low-field (<1T). It trains on repeated MRI acquisitions without ground truth data, enabling noise-adaptive training and improving generalization across varying noise levels. The approach outperforms existing methods like MC-SURE in terms of preserving structural details and reducing residual noise.

top_models.title: Adaptive Deep Learning MR Image Enhancement with Property Constrained Unrolled Network
top_models.key_method: Proximal Gradient Descent, Unrolled Network, Property Constraints
top_models.summary: PGDNet, a hybrid approach combining proximal gradient descent and unrolling networks, is proposed for adaptive MRI image denoising and deblurring. It uses property constraints on the degradation kernel to improve performance.

top_models.title: Multispectral nonlocal means filters incorporating rotations and reflections for improved noise reduction with edge preservation in magnetic resonance imaging
top_models.summary: The paper presents a multispectral nonlocal means filter (MS-NLM) incorporating rotations and reflections to improve noise reduction while preserving edges in MRI images. The approach compares local neighborhoods of voxels after rotation and reflection, enhancing the use of image redundancy.

top_models.title: Deep learning without ground truth: an unsupervised method for MR image denoising and super-resolution
top_models.summary: The study demonstrates an unsupervised learning approach for MR denoising and super-resolution by leveraging the architectural bias of convolutional neural networks (Deep Image Prior) as an image constraint. This method eliminates the need for paired ground-truth images, relying instead on network parameter optimization.

top_models.title: Deform-Mamba Network for MRI Super-Resolution
top_models.key_method: Deformable convolution and vision Mamba block encoder
top_models.summary: The paper introduces Deform-Mamba, a novel architecture combining deformable convolution and State Space Models for efficient MRI super-resolution. It addresses the computational limitations of Transformers while effectively capturing both local and global image features.

top_models.title: SCORE-BASED SELF-SUPERVISED MRI DENOISING
top_models.summary: Corruption2Self (C2S) is a self-supervised MRI denoising framework that uses generalized denoising score matching to learn from noisy observations without clean ground truth. It effectively balances noise reduction and feature preservation using a reparameterized noise-level strategy.

top_models.title: AgentMRI: A Vision Language Model-Powered AI System for Self-regulating MRI Reconstruction with Multiple Degradations
top_models.key_method: Multi-query VLM strategy for robust corruption detection and automatic selection of deep learning models for MRI reconstruction
top_models.summary: AgentMRI is an AI-driven system leveraging vision language models to perform fully autonomous MRI reconstruction in the presence of multiple degradations, dynamically selecting the best correction model without manual intervention.

top_models.title: MR image super-resolution via a variational diffusion model
top_models.key_method: Variational Diffusion Model with guided k-space sampling
top_models.summary: This paper proposes a new MR image super-resolution method using a variational diffusion model, which leverages lower-resolution K-space measurements to guide the generation process without retraining.
top_models.model_name: Variational Diffusion Model with guided k-space sampling
